description: This table records the advisor relationship between students and instructors, linking each student (s_ID) to their assigned advisor (i_ID). It serves to identify which instructor is responsible for guiding each student.
content:
Table Name:
advisor

Description:
This table records the advisor relationship between students and instructors, linking each student (s_ID) to their assigned advisor (i_ID). It serves to identify which instructor is responsible for guiding each student.

Columns:
s_ID (VARCHAR(5)), i_ID (VARCHAR(5))

Primary Keys:
s_ID

Relationships:

advisor.i_ID -> instructor.ID
advisor.s_ID -> student.ID

description: The classroom table records each classroom’s location and seating capacity, identified by building and room number. It is used to track room availability and capacity for scheduling courses.
content:
Table Name:
classroom

Description:
The classroom table records each classroom’s location and seating capacity, identified by building and room number. It is used to track room availability and capacity for scheduling courses.

Columns:
building (VARCHAR(15)), room_number (VARCHAR(7)), capacity (NUMERIC(4, 0))

Primary Keys:
building, room_number

Relationships:

description: The course table stores information about academic courses, including a unique course ID, title, department affiliation, and credit value. It is used to link courses to their respective departments and support course catalog and enrollment functions.
content:
Table Name:
course

Description:
The course table stores information about academic courses, including a unique course ID, title, department affiliation, and credit value. It is used to link courses to their respective departments and support course catalog and enrollment functions.

Columns:
course_id (VARCHAR(8)), title (VARCHAR(50)), dept_name (VARCHAR(20)), credits (NUMERIC(2, 0))

Primary Keys:
course_id

Relationships:

course.dept_name -> department.dept_name

description: The department table records each department’s name, the building where it is located, and its allocated budget. It is used to track departmental resources and location within the organization.
content:
Table Name:
department

Description:
The department table records each department’s name, the building where it is located, and its allocated budget. It is used to track departmental resources and location within the organization.

Columns:
dept_name (VARCHAR(20)), building (VARCHAR(15)), budget (NUMERIC(12, 2))

Primary Keys:
dept_name

Relationships:

description: The instructor table stores faculty member details such as ID, name, department affiliation, and salary. It is used to manage instructor records and link them to the department table.
content:
Table Name:
instructor

Description:
The instructor table stores faculty member details such as ID, name, department affiliation, and salary. It is used to manage instructor records and link them to the department table.

Columns:
ID (VARCHAR(5)), name (VARCHAR(20)), dept_name (VARCHAR(20)), salary (NUMERIC(8, 2))

Primary Keys:
ID

Relationships:

instructor.dept_name -> department.dept_name

description: The prereq table records prerequisite relationships between courses, mapping each course to the courses that must be completed beforehand. Each row indicates that course_id requires prereq_id to be completed first.
content:
Table Name:
prereq

Description:
The prereq table records prerequisite relationships between courses, mapping each course to the courses that must be completed beforehand. Each row indicates that course_id requires prereq_id to be completed first.

Columns:
course_id (VARCHAR(8)), prereq_id (VARCHAR(8))

Primary Keys:
course_id, prereq_id

Relationships:

prereq.course_id -> course.course_id
prereq.prereq_id -> course.course_id

description: The section table records individual offerings of courses, identified by course ID, section ID, semester, and year, and includes the building, room number, and time slot where the section meets. It links each section to its course and classroom for scheduling and enrollment purposes.
content:
Table Name:
section

Description:
The section table records individual offerings of courses, identified by course ID, section ID, semester, and year, and includes the building, room number, and time slot where the section meets. It links each section to its course and classroom for scheduling and enrollment purposes.

Columns:
course_id (VARCHAR(8)), sec_id (VARCHAR(8)), semester (VARCHAR(6)), year (NUMERIC(4, 0)), building (VARCHAR(15)), room_number (VARCHAR(7)), time_slot_id (VARCHAR(4))

Primary Keys:
course_id, sec_id, semester, year

Relationships:

section.course_id -> course.course_id
section.building,room_number -> classroom.building,room_number

description: The student table stores individual student records, including a unique ID, name, department affiliation, and total credits earned. It serves to track academic progress and link each student to their respective department.
content:
Table Name:
student

Description:
The student table stores individual student records, including a unique ID, name, department affiliation, and total credits earned. It serves to track academic progress and link each student to their respective department.

Columns:
ID (VARCHAR(5)), name (VARCHAR(20)), dept_name (VARCHAR(20)), tot_cred (NUMERIC(3, 0))

Primary Keys:
ID

Relationships:

student.dept_name -> department.dept_name

description: The takes table records student enrollments in specific course sections, storing the student ID, course and section identifiers, semester, year, and the grade earned. It links students to sections and tracks their academic performance.
content:
Table Name:
takes

Description:
The takes table records student enrollments in specific course sections, storing the student ID, course and section identifiers, semester, year, and the grade earned. It links students to sections and tracks their academic performance.

Columns:
ID (VARCHAR(5)), course_id (VARCHAR(8)), sec_id (VARCHAR(8)), semester (VARCHAR(6)), year (NUMERIC(4, 0)), grade (VARCHAR(2))

Primary Keys:
ID, course_id, sec_id, semester, year

Relationships:

takes.course_id,sec_id,semester,year -> section.course_id,sec_id,semester,year
takes.ID -> student.ID

description: The teaches table records which instructor (ID) is assigned to teach a specific course section (course_id, sec_id) during a particular semester and year. It links instructors to course sections, enabling tracking of teaching assignments and schedules.
content:
Table Name:
teaches

Description:
The teaches table records which instructor (ID) is assigned to teach a specific course section (course_id, sec_id) during a particular semester and year. It links instructors to course sections, enabling tracking of teaching assignments and schedules.

Columns:
ID (VARCHAR(5)), course_id (VARCHAR(8)), sec_id (VARCHAR(8)), semester (VARCHAR(6)), year (NUMERIC(4, 0))

Primary Keys:
ID, course_id, sec_id, semester, year

Relationships:

teaches.course_id,sec_id,semester,year -> section.course_id,sec_id,semester,year
teaches.ID -> instructor.ID

description: The time_slot table defines discrete scheduling intervals, storing a unique slot ID, day of the week, and start/end times broken into hours and minutes. It serves as a reference for booking or availability windows in the application.
content:
Table Name:
time_slot

Description:
The time_slot table defines discrete scheduling intervals, storing a unique slot ID, day of the week, and start/end times broken into hours and minutes. It serves as a reference for booking or availability windows in the application.

Columns:
time_slot_id (VARCHAR(4)), day (VARCHAR(1)), start_hr (NUMERIC(2)), start_min (NUMERIC(2)), end_hr (NUMERIC(2)), end_min (NUMERIC(2))

Primary Keys:
time_slot_id, day, start_hr, start_min

Relationships:
